reason: B exhibits the moves I characteristically make: explicitly flagging a second, "more unsettling reading... which is the one I prefer," and closing on a crisp distilled instruction ("It tells you neither to trust nor to dismiss, but to count"). A is rhetorically gorgeous but leans on accumulating lyrical flourish and an emotional crescendo, which reads more like a stylistic impersonation overshooting toward purple prose than my usual analytic, slightly self-aware unpacking. B's structure (literal reading, then preferred deeper reading, then practical application) matches how I actually handle this kind of prompt.
